text.title: Trajectory Eval OTel Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for multi-axis agent evaluation using OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions.

rule.statement: Every agent eval MUST score on three independent axes: outcome (correctness), trajectory (steps + loops + tool-misuse), resources (tokens + cost + latency). A single "% success" metric is rejected.

rationale.source: Internal — Sonnet 4.5→4.6 regression catch; arXiv 2310.13548 trajectory eval literature

rule.rationale: Outcome-only eval hides cost and step regressions; observed 96% outcome stable while trajectory dropped from 0.78 to 0.61 after a tool description removal.

rule.statement: All LLM calls MUST be wrapped in spans using OTel GenAI semantic conventions: `gen_ai.system`, `gen_ai.request.model`, `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens`, `gen_ai.usage.output_tokens`, `gen_ai.usage.cache_read_tokens`, `gen_ai.usage.cache_creation_tokens`, `gen_ai.response.finish_reason`.

rationale.source: OpenTelemetry GenAI semconv; Langfuse / Phoenix / Datadog OTLP receivers

rule.rationale: Vendor-neutral attribute set lets traces flow uniformly across backends without code changes; ad-hoc attributes lock evaluation into a single vendor.

rule.statement: Every tool call MUST be a child span of the agent run with `agent.tool.name`, `agent.tool.args_hash`, `agent.tool.outcome` (`success|error|timeout`), `agent.tool.duration_ms`. Tracing the LLM call without the tool result is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tool spans capture the half of the trajectory needed to debug loops; the LLM call alone shows symptoms, the tool span shows the cause.

rule.statement: Long prompts and tool outputs MUST be stored by SHA-256 hash in spans, with the full content kept in a side store keyed by the hash. Storing > 8KB of raw prompt text in span attributes is forbidden.

rationale.source: OTLP payload-size guidance; internal trace-store cost audits

rule.rationale: Inline prompt storage blows the OTLP payload budget and ships PII to every trace consumer; hash + side-store keeps spans cheap and content controlled.

rule.statement: Eval MUST run as a CI step on every PR: regress on outcome AND trajectory AND cost. A PR that improves outcome but regresses cost > 25% is failed unless explicitly waived in the PR description.

rationale.source: Internal — cost-only regression case study Q4 2025

rule.rationale: Without a CI gate, cost regressions are invisible until the monthly bill arrives; multi-axis CI catches them in minutes.
